review_text: Stayed in a lovely cabin ( double story) right on the beautiful Kendall’s beach. The accommodation was an enjoyable fifteen minute walk into town and was clean , spacious and comfortable with lots of windows to enjoy the   gorgeous view. The beds were really comfortable and the kitchen had every  amenity needed. The staff were delightful and I would highly recommend to stay here.
This park is the best all amenities are so clean plus you couldn’t get a nicer  couple running kendalls beach holiday park Thankyou for your hospitality 🥝🤗

Camped here w caravan for the long weekend in March. Beach is nice although not good for young kids with waves crashing right in beach. Toilets showers are nice and clean but they require a key instead of a code. Camp kitchen has standard stuff. Camp has lots of cabins and only 15 camp-sites. Lady owner is a bit of a dragon.

We stayed here for one night in our camper in February 2026. Unfortunately, the park’s location directly by the beach is the only positive thing worth mentioning.

Our site had a water connection that was practically unreachable, and the amenities were only moderately clean. During our stay, there were also a few guests with dogs that appeared somewhat dangerous.

The biggest issue, however — as already mentioned by other reviewers — is that the park seems to have a serious problem with its immediate neighbors, who blast extremely loud music across the entire campground all day long. It was obvious that these rather antisocial-seeming neighbors were doing this deliberately to harass the guests.

It is hard to believe that the park management is unaware of this situation.

The management urgently needs to find a solution with the neighbors; otherwise, the park cannot be recommended at all.
Just had our first visit, excellent location, wonderful park managers, very clean amenities and walking distance into town. We will return

Yup, it's right on the beach. Toilets and bathrooms could do with a spruce up though. Shame it's got public access to the beach carpark. Bigger than usual pitches, but there's grass on all. Would return in the off season.

We stayed at this campsite with our caravan and used an ensuite site, which was clean and a good size. The facilities themselves were good and had the potential to make for a very relaxing stay.

Unfortunately, what really lets this caravan park down is the neighbours behind site 54 (not fellow campers). There was very loud music played from around 6–8 pm on one evening, and again on Saturday from about 3–8 pm. One camper politely asked them to turn it down, but instead the music was played even louder.

This completely ruined what could have been a peaceful weekend. It’s a real shame, as otherwise the park itself was fine. Because of this experience, we would probably not return.

If you’re looking for a relaxing stay, I’d strongly recommend avoiding sites near 54 or choosing a different park altogether.Review of campsite to review

We stayed at this campsite with our caravan and used an ensuite site, which was clean and a good size. The facilities themselves were good and had the potential to make for a very relaxing stay.

Unfortunately, what really lets this caravan park down is the neighbours behind site 54 (not fellow campers). There was very loud music played from around 6–8 pm on one evening, and again on Saturday from about 3–8 pm. One camper politely asked them to turn it down, but instead the music was played even louder.

This completely ruined what could have been a peaceful weekend. It’s a real shame, as otherwise the park itself was fine. Because of this experience, we would probably not return.

If you’re looking for a relaxing stay, I’d strongly recommend avoiding sites near 54 or choosing a different park altogether.

We recently stayed for our 21st year at Kendall’s . We love the park , it is always well maintained and the amenities are spotless .
Management and staff are friendly , welcoming and helpful .
We have rebooked , of course .
Awesome spot great amenities great people beautiful beach ⛱️⛱️

My wife came down with Covid a couple days before and was feeling horrible. Her whole body was in pain. Reception lady didn't let us reschedule. We'd get charged half the cancellation fee. She made it out like she was doing us a favour. Basically they dont care if their guests are exposed to people with covid. Book with them at your own peril.

This was our return visit, and it was just as wonderful as the first time! We stayed on an ensuite caravan site, which was excellent – clean, well-maintained, and added that extra level of comfort.
We travelled with my partner and our dog, and it’s genuinely pet-friendly – dogs are not just allowed, they’re truly welcome.
The park is beautifully leafy and incredibly relaxing, with a calm atmosphere that makes it easy to unwind. It’s only a short stroll to the beach, and there’s a walking track that takes you straight to Kiama shops and amenities. While there’s no shop on-site, local shops are just a 10-minute walk away, which is very convenient.
Overall, this is the ideal place if you’re looking for peace, nature, and convenience. We’ll definitely be back again!

I really liked this place  ,everything was great exept for resident that lives on the back fence that loves loud music,2 of the 3 nights it was loud enough we had to yell to communicate and couldn't hear our TV. So I suggest if you want a peaceful holiday the ensuite powered sites are is not good between 5 & 10pm

Our caravan site was amazing! Very close to beach which we loved listening to the sound of the waves going to sleep- pure bliss, after 10pm when noise curfew was in place! |Only negative was the inconsiderate home owner who lives behind the caravan sites who listened to music blaring for all the park to hear!!! Even with all our windows shut we still could hear the loud music- one night we didn’t open our windows till 10pm when the home owners turned the music off.
Only small, very narrow to get in but the do have caravan spots close to exit
Love the spot

Located right on the beach I stayed in a roof top tent. Other family stayed in the cabins and a caravan. Amenities were clean as well as the park. Office staff were helpful and the prices were good. Only downfall were the local kids hanging around the park/beach drunk and causing a nuisance to anyone who were camped on the outskirts of the park. Not the owners fault but it did cause a couple of sleepless nights.

A perfect gem of a park. Located beachfront with full views of the bay just walk out of your door to the stunning quiet beach. Everything was spot on, the real strength are the management team, we stayed twice and both times they went out of their way to make our stay just what we wanted, so friendly and welcoming.

Excellent park Very clean and safe
Cabins Excellent Plenty of room.. Clean Well stocked and all rooms good size
Great location right by the beach
Very friendly staff
Overall one of the best caravan parks ee have stayed in
Great time had by the patrolling members of Cronulla SLSC.  Highly recommended for big groups
Handy for our stay in Kiama

We have stayed at Kendall’s On The Beach Holiday Park during the October long weekend for the past 4 years. We have also rebooked for the 2026 long weekend as well.

We love the location and the facilities. We personally choose to stay in a cabin because the cabins are beach front and we are not keen on camping. All cabins have a designated parking space for one car. Linen is provided in the cabins but you should still bring beach towels. The cabin we stay in, is fitted with gas cooking, a full size fridge, small dishwasher and a barbecue.

Camping spots are not beach front but still close to the beach. The facilities of the holiday park are always clean and well maintained.

There is a public children’s play park that has a slide and swings not too far from the beach and water.

The beach is within walking distance from your door regardless of what accommodation option you choose within the park.

The holiday park is within walking distance from the Main Street and a second beach that is patrolled but you might need to check patrolling times.

The walk to the Main Street of Kiama is mostly via designated footpaths. It’s is a moderate walk that requires some uphill walking.

There is a fair selection of restaurants and pubs within walking distance along with lots of food delivery options.

The whole park is child friendly and we have always found the noise levels to be acceptable  during our stay.

What an absolute gem of a place.
Gorgeous Beach, great location, good people, lots of walks and places to see.
This is a MUST see location.
I just love it hear.
Oh oh and the on site cabins are spectacular

Our stay at Kendall’s beach was amazing. The place was so well equipped and so clean. The staff were very friendly and the facilities were great. We stayed in the 3 bedroom which was nice and big. The beach is right there and it’s an easy walk into the centre of town. Highly recommend, we will be back.

A lovely holiday park. We had a motorhome and kept extending our stay. Was low season so plenty or room, right near the beach.  Staff are very friendly, lovely and clean modern amenities. The camp kitchen has a fully enclosed area which was great as it rained a few days in a row. Good drainage so not muddy either. Highly recommended

Beautiful location, team are very friendly and nice, the cabin was immaculate and very comfortable. Our family are very happy with our winter weekend away, and would highly recommend. We will definitely stay again in the near future. Thank you
Very clean, Serviceable cabins, have everything you need. Friendly staff. Great amenities,  close to beach. Close to town.

Even with the wintery weather, we had an amazing time here! Pam on reception was extremely friendly & overall lovely to deal with. The camp kitchen was clean, tidy, and had all essential equipment. Bathrooms were immaculate. The park is well designed & directly on the beach and only a 30 minute into town/blowhole! Definitely recommend Kendalls on The Beach Holiday Park! We will be back in the warmer weather to enjoy the park/beach more with the sunshine!!!

Had a powered site over the long weekend
Great weekend with well kept clean facilities. See you guys again soon
The view was fantastic. The cabin was very well appointed. The staff were very friendly. I would highly recommend this holiday park. We had a great getaway 😊
Wonderful location right on the beach. Very good camp kitchen and dining area. Clean spacious toilets and lovely hot showers, what else could some campers want on a winters night.

Had an amazing holiday with our daughter and her partner and, of course, our grandchildren.
Great location and clean amenities.
The location is perfect for the beach and not far to walk into the township of Kiama.
Will come back for a longer holiday.

Fabulous spot ! beach front cabins well appointed and serviced with good onsite ammenities.
Highly recommend for people working away in the area or holidaying.
Shops cafes pubs etc only a
Couple of minutes away.
A coffee Van comes to site also.
Great holiday spot

Always love it here. Melissa, Pam and Sue are so welcoming and make our stay enjoyable. The facilities, cabins and sites are very clean and comfortable. Nothing is ever a problem. Will be back again and again.
Amazing 🌹 Excellent

Location superb. Amenities spotless BUT all sites are the same price ( $60) regardless of whether your site has torn matting,is uneven and your view is the row of 12 bins  ..OR the delightfully landscaped slabs ,many with picnic tables.
Doesn't seem equitable.

Site 60 and 61 are seriously below par for the price.
Great place with beach in front

Great cabins. We had a beach cabin. Slept 5. Clean. Great view. Huge bathroom, one double bed  and 3 bunks ,large living area , well fitted out. Beach only metres away, had a grass area  between cabin and beach. Not far from town centre.  Gerringong  not far, Berry only 20 minutes.

Everything about this site is positive—well-kept, tidy, and in an excellent location.

However, for tent campers, there’s a small drawback. I stayed 11 nights at a premium rate but was placed in the least ideal spot for sunny days (and there were many). Despite requesting a shady site, I was given a standard policy response and left with no option—spot #49 is best avoided.

Overall, it was a beautiful stay, but a lingering sense of being overlooked left a slight mark. For longer stays outside winter, I’d recommend choosing a park that takes the time to listen to guest requests.

This time, the art of negotiation wasn’t in my favor—better luck next time!

This park may be in a great location. However, we arrived in a motorhome and were assigned spot 41, which was so close to number 40 that our slide-out was under their awning. Be cautious when booking.

Excellent caravan park. Amenities were very spacious and clean. Less than 50m walk to a lovely beach. The walk into town is not to bad (a little hilly) but doable. It took us around 20 mins. We would definitely return.

Camp sites are a little crowded.
Facilities are clean and spacious.
Proximity to the beach is great.

Our stay had some highlights, but also a few issues worth noting for future guests. First, the location is absolutely beautiful, with easy access to great spots around Kiama, plus nearby options for dining and groceries, which made things very convenient. The cabin itself was clean and well-maintained, so it’s clear they take care of the facilities.

However, there were some aspects of our experience that were unexpected. We booked in advance during the 2023-2024 financial year, assuming the quoted price would stay fixed, but were informed that rates could change by our stay in the 2024-2025 financial year. When we reached out to ask about it, the response felt rather terse and didn’t offer much clarity.

Inside the cabin, while it was very tidy, the lighting was bright and clinical, with a flickering fluorescent above the bed in the second bedroom that only worked when adjusted. A softer, warmer lighting setup or bedside lamps would have made the space feel more inviting. There was also no fan in the second room, and even with windows open, the room stayed stuffy due to the cabin’s layout limiting airflow.

Lastly, only one parking spot was provided per cabin, which was challenging since we had two couples travelling from different locations in NSW.

Overall, we enjoyed the beautiful setting and found the location convenient, but a few adjustments could make the stay even more comfortable and guest-friendly.
Great holiday park right on the beach. $45 for 2 of us to stay on an unpowered site, which is great considering the location!
Very nice place, a little bit expensive but everything is clean (amenities) and working.
Such a wonderful place! Great for families, picnics, or just enjoying nature of Kiama.

Nice place on the beach with excellent welcoming staff. The area is asking $60pn for a powered site which is up there. Easy walk into town.
Reply. Seeing as we stayed on a Thursday night, you are welcome
Spent the weekend at Kendall’s on the Beach.  2 nights on a powered site.  A very quiet and straightforward park.  Right on the beach.  We’ll be back

Our first visit to Kiama & and the first visit to Kendall's On The Beach.
What a fantastic park facility.
Highly recommend & will definitely be coming back with friends.
A huge thankyou to the staff on checking in. My wife walked in after being on the road for hrs & were immediately accommodated within the park & on the front.

My cabin number 19 the disabled access was quite nice my bed was abit high  my liking the Bunks in the second room was quite small there wasn't much room the disabled bathroom was okay but floor was quite slippery when wet and was scared to slip on the tiles the dining room was large but lounges was uncomfortable at times when I have arthritis in my spine

This park is in prime position, absolute beach frontage. Amenities are 5 star and well maintained, along with the grounds. A huge camp kitchen with indoor and outdoor BBQ and sink. Staff are very helpful and friendly. Would definitely stay again.

Stayed a couple of nights in a Garden Spa Cabin. So warm and cosy even though the breeze was chilly. Glimpse of the sun on the water from our cabin. Right on such a stunning little beach. Great location for walks into town and both Blowholes. We will be back with our own caravan next time and stay longer
lovely spot. i went for a little 2 night getaway. Cabin had everything needed and was very cosy and comfortable.

Place is amazing. Great for young families. Watch the sunrise in the morning. Beach at front. Playground for young kids. Close drive to Blow hole and main shops.Would recommend and stay at this place again.
Awesome

Nice park, we had a site whuch backed the clothes line which was fine. Big sites with heaps of room.  Staff were helpful. Amenities were clean and showers good. They had a long mirror away from the sinks where you could dry your hair or do your make-up with a decent bench.  Overall this place was great with a lovely beach and great walks around the foreshore. We used it as a base to sightsee. We would definitely stay there again.
Absolutely stunning.

Beautiful!!
We loved our stay at Kendalls, it was cozy and cabins so welcoming. The kids made ample use of the washer and dryer, the sand is next level!
Great cabins, lovely surrounds, friendly and helpful service. Thank you for your support in amending our situation, your professionalism was appreciated.
Beautiful place nice and clean
It was great. Close to the beach. Short walk to Kiama.

We have been taking our caravan to Kendall's for Easter and October long weekends for quite a few years now. It's a fabulous place to stay. Staff super friendly and very helpful.  The camp kitchen has been a recent addition in the last few years and is one of the best I've seen. Highly recommend. Hope to be coming back for years to come.
The perfect family holiday park!
Awesome spot
Beautiful spot short walk into kiama
Had a booking on arrival but needed a pet friendly cabin due to bad weather, nothing available but staff help us find something at a different park.

From the initial (multiple lol) phone calls with Pam prior to booking, the ease of communication, the warm, friendly greeting on arrival, the stunning cabin and the even more stunning view from our verandah, right up until our departure, everything was AWESOME!! We may have only stayed for the weekend, but we left feeling as though we had been away for a week, in a good way of course. :)

We would not hesitate to stay here again, or to recommend this site to friends or family.

Amazing! We were so lucky with our unpowered site to have ocean views, but all are close to the water and an easy walk to the beach
Reception staff were so lovely, amenities were always clean and well equipped
We would definitely stay again:)

Great holiday Park. Right on beachfront.Very clean and excellent facilities.
Highly recommend!
Perfect spot you feel like you’re on your own little island

The location of this place is second to none in regards to beach access. The view is first class. The cabins are clean and modest but perfectly fine for a nice stay on the beach. We have booked to go back in a few months. We love this place for a family getaway close to home.

This is a stunning camp not very big but  immaculately located within the city centre of Kiama ||It’s  got a massive beach surrounded by hills on all sides,  just a fascinating location with a sparkling kitchen toilets and a well equipped reception
Like it
It's really awesome
Great place on the beach, showers and toilets are clean, not far from town, friendly service

Cabin accommodation excellent. Immaculately clean & well presented cabin. Fully contained unit had everything you could need. Beds were comfortable. Close to the beach and the park was also immaculately clean and presented. Real nice place to sit back and just relax.  The spa bath was fantastic.

Update 2023 - $55/night, 2 people, campervan, stayed for 3 nights. Book in advance, they are popular.  Staff were great, friendly, helpful and genuinely nice. We had a site off to the back by the walking path but was comfortable, great wifi. It's a bit of a walk to the amenities but easily accessible and they are clean. Camp kitchen has 2 BBQs with a sink, 4 burner cooktop, counter, sink, kettle, toaster and microwave. Can't beat the seating area indoor and out. Perfectly situated to walk into town or to many of the attractions in the area.

Only hiccup was s group of teens camped next to us one night who weren't as quiet as they thought they were. Security was called by another party and it was handled.

Highly recommend and would stay again.

$40/night for 2 people, campervan, powered site. Nice place, reception and staff very friendly. Amenities are clean and well maintained, newer. The camp kitchen is nice, well set up with two BBQ'S and a gas cooktop along with all the other items. The only issue here is that the hood vents need cleaned, there is a massive grease buildup on the screens. Otherwise it's clean and we'll maintained. The sites are nice and you get good space. You can easily walk to either blowhole and into town from here. Would stay again.
Very nice park. Clean amenities, staff very friendly and nothing was an issue. Beautiful spot.
One nite, clean amenities.
Fabulous weekend away. Beach front cabins were awesome!

Beautiful stay at Kendalls Beach. We were upgraded to cabins right on the beach which had everything we needed and a late check out of 4pm. Pam was very helpful and made sure our families were looked after. Thank you.

We stayed here from last Saturday to Wednesday for our first family holiday! It was a nice walk to the street from the cabins. We stayed in room 7 which wasnt the “waterfront views” but from the master bedroom/balcony and verandah you could see the ocean perfectly!! Very clean room, provided a high chair for us which was really handy! Very nice and helpful staff will 100% stay again in the future!!
Perfect for all aged kids with walks, beach and playground. Beach front cabins perfect for weekend getaway.
Very friendly staff and beautiful location and dog friendly. It has a lovely camp kitchen and easy access to the beach. Would come back and recommend it.
It was very nice. Not much room though. Everyone too close to each other...but a stunning location.  Showers, toilets extremely clean.

Very nice small caravan park immaculate amenities, newly renovated camp kitchen, easy walk into Kiama along the pathway at the front of the park and most importantly to us it’s dog friendly. The beach is beautiful but not dog friendly, there is a small off leash area on the headland.
The staff were very friendly and accommodating
Taste the salt- nice and close - well worth it - book early
